first_paragraph: [Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding_at_the_Winter_Olympics "Snowboarding at the Winter Olympics") at the [2022 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2022_Winter_Olympics "2022 Winter Olympics") were held at the [Genting Snow Park](/wiki/Genting_Snow_Park "Genting Snow Park") in [Zhangjiakou](/wiki/Zhangjiakou "Zhangjiakou") and [Big Air Shougang](/wiki/Big_Air_Shougang "Big Air Shougang") in [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), [China](/wiki/China "China"). The events took place between 5 and 15 February 2022. A total of 11 snowboarding events were held.[[1]](#cite_note-schedule-1)

[Snowboarding](/wiki/Snowboarding_at_the_Winter_Olympics "Snowboarding at the Winter Olympics") at the [2022 Winter Olympics](/wiki/2022_Winter_Olympics "2022 Winter Olympics") were held at the [Genting Snow Park](/wiki/Genting_Snow_Park "Genting Snow Park") in [Zhangjiakou](/wiki/Zhangjiakou "Zhangjiakou") and [Big Air Shougang](/wiki/Big_Air_Shougang "Big Air Shougang") in [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing"), [China](/wiki/China "China"). The events took place between 5 and 15 February 2022. A total of 11 snowboarding events were held.[[1]](#cite_note-schedule-1)

In July 2018, the [International Olympic Committee](/wiki/International_Olympic_Committee "International Olympic Committee") (IOC) officially added the mixed team snowboard cross event to the Olympic program, increasing the total number of events to 11.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

A total of 238 quota spots were distributed to the sport of snowboarding, a decline of 20 from the 2018 Winter Olympics.[[4]](#cite_note-4) A total of 11 events were contested, five for men, five for women and one mixed.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Qualification

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Snowboarding_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Qualification")]

Main article: [Snowboarding at the 2022 Winter Olympics – Qualification](/wiki/Snowboarding_at_the_2022_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Qualification "Snowboarding at the 2022 Winter Olympics – Qualification")

A total of 238 athletes qualified to compete in the snowboarding events (119 men and 119 women). A country can enter a maximum of 26 athletes across all events, with a maximum of 14 per gender. A total of eight quotas (one per event) is reserved for the host nation, if it fails to qualify in that respective event. Each event also has a minimum FIS points total required per athlete, along with a top 30 finish at a World Cup event during the qualification period (1 July 2019 or 2020 in Parallel giant slalom, to 16 January 2022), or [2021 FIS Snowboarding World Championships](/wiki/FIS_Freestyle_Ski_and_Snowboarding_World_Championships_2021 "FIS Freestyle Ski and Snowboarding World Championships 2021").[[6]](#cite_note-a-6) A total of 16 NOC's will qualify for the mixed team snowboard cross event.[[6]](#cite_note-a-6) The athlete quota per event is listed below.

| Event | Men | Women | Minimum FIS points |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Big air/Slopestyle | 30 | 30 | 50.00 |
| Halfpipe | 25 | 25 | 50.00 |
| Parallel giant slalom | 32 | 32 | 100.00 |
| Snowboard cross | 32 | 32 | 100.00 |
| 238 quotas | 119 | 119 |  |

* Big air and slopestyle have a combined event quota.
